questions.prompt: Why use PaymentIntents instead of the older Charges API?

choices.text: PaymentIntents are a state machine that natively handles modern flows: 3D Secure / SCA challenges, asynchronous payment methods (bank transfers, BNPL), off-session retries, and multi-step authorizations. Charges are single-shot and can't represent these cleanly. Stripe recommends PaymentIntents for all new integrations.

questions.explanation: Modern payments have multi-step lifecycles (3DS, async confirmations, retries). The Charge object was single-shot and couldn't model these cleanly. PaymentIntents are the modern primitive — use them for everything new.

questions.prompt: A subscription auto-renewal off-session fails with `requires_action`. What's the right response?

choices.text: The card needs 3DS authentication; the user must be present to complete it. Surface a banner / email: 'Action required on your card; click to authenticate.' When the user clicks, complete the PaymentIntent on-session via Stripe.js with `confirmPayment`. After authentication, the subscription resumes. Don't try to bypass — issuer requires user-present authentication.

questions.explanation: Off-session payments that need 3DS can't be completed without the user. The pattern: surface a clear 'action required' UX, walk the user through authentication on their next visit, then resume the subscription. Repeated off-session retries don't fix it.
